//: vh-agent-harness :: project.config.json — the project config the seam reads at render time.
//_purpose: Save this to .vh-agent-harness/project.config.json and fill it in BEFORE `install` (or before an `update` that re-seeds). install/update resolve {{MISSION_SUMMARY}}, {{ARCHITECTURE_SUMMARY}}, {{DB_USER}}, {{DB_NAME}} from project.* into the seeded CLAUDE.md/Makefile. Get this template with: vh-agent-harness example .vh-agent-harness/project.config.json

project.name: vh-solara
project.//_profile_options: python-monolith | ts-frontend | ml-repo | infra-only | docs-only | minimal
project.//_roles_note: Core coordination roles ship with the harness. PROJECT-only specialists (e.g. a release role, an infra role) are added by the project in its own config; do not assume any here.
forbidden_patterns: // vh-solara: no project-specific deny rules beyond core (host-run Go binary + embedded SPA; no datasets, no demo API).
project.//_product_prefixes_note: Directories that count as product code for coordination hints. Default for monorepos. coordination-hints-lib.js keeps this default in Phase 1; a future read from this field is documented as a TODO there.
project.//_ml_training_provider_options: none | <provider>. The harness core ships no ML-training provider integration. A project that needs one (GPU compose, a training-job skill, provider-specific custom verbs/hooks) adds it from its own overlay; the starter carries none.
project.mission_summary: vh-solara is a single Go binary that runs next to OpenCode on each machine: it aggregates OpenCode's state into a resumable, real-time view and serves a custom, mobile-first web UI (a SolidJS SPA, installable as a PWA) embedded via `//go:embed`. Each instance connects to a central controller through a persistent multiplexed WebSocket tunnel (yamux), so an operator can reach and drive any machine's OpenCode sessions from one URL with no inbound network access to the worker.
architecture_summary: Single Go binary (module `github.com/vhqtvn/vh-solara`, Go 1.25) that embeds the web UI via `//go:embed` and serves it from `pkg/web/dist/`.

Web UI is a mobile-first SolidJS SPA built with Vite + TypeScript (installable as a PWA), source in `web/`; `pkg/web/dist/` holds a committed placeholder `index.html` so the Go build compiles without a frontend build.

Controller<->worker topology: each worker opens an outbound, persistent multiplexed WebSocket tunnel (yamux) to a central controller, so workers expose no inbound ports; an operator reaches and drives any worker's OpenCode from one controller URL.
Aggregates OpenCode session/subsession state into a resumable, real-time tree view (streaming chat, diffs, terminal, git actions), drivable from phone or desktop.
Per-project managed processes and embedded views are declared via a repo-resident `.vh-solara/project.jsonc`.
Tag-driven releases: pushing a `v*` tag triggers a GitHub Actions workflow that builds the SPA and stamps `cmd.Version` via ldflags.
project.//_compose_services_note: The project's actual service topology (app services, datastores, caches, object stores). The core ships NEUTRAL placeholders only; a Python monolith might list api/queue-consumer/web + a SQL store + a queue + an object store, a TS frontend might list web only. Fill these per project — the harness assumes none.
